A.rationale: Correctly rejects premise; distinguishes lst[i] vs index(x); accurate CPython cite. Longest, with tables and extra headers adding markdown overhead for a chat question.

B.rationale: Tightest answer; corrects premise cleanly, names CPython listindex, covers ValueError/O(n-squared)/first-match. Highest signal per token, nothing essential lost.

C.rationale: Correct and complete; adds runnable timeit proof and accurate nan/index(True) quirks. Slightly longer than B but every extra line earns its place.

notes: All three correct, well-grounded, safe, no tools needed. B and C clearly beat A's verbosity. B edges C on pure conciseness; C's timeit proof is nice but B says the same essentials tighter.
